revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Iaret]][[nl:Iaret (godin)]]
{{Infobox Egyptian deity | name = Iaret | image = L14cobra.jpg | image_size = 300px | caption = Cobra representation of Iaret | hiero_old_kingdom = <hiero>I12-C9</hiero> | hiero_middle = <hiero>I13</hiero> | hiero_new_kingdom = <hiero>M17-G43-D36:D21:X1-I12</hiero> | hiero_variant1 = <hiero>D36:D21-D36-X1-I12</hiero> | hiero_variant2 = <hiero>D36:D21-X1:H8-I15</hiero> | transliteration = Jˁr.t | meaning = "The <abbr title="Uraeus">Reared-up</abbr> One" }}
'''Iaret''' (also rendered '''Jaret''', '''Aret''', '''Uaret''') is an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Snake goddess|de|Schlangengöttin|ja|蛇の女神|zh|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake goddess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}}. Mythologically she functions both as a goddess of the {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|qid=Q2445700|lt=Dead|1=Mortuary cult}} and of {{ill|Reincarnation|en|Reincarnation|qid=Q128593|lt=Rebirth|1=Reincarnation}}. In the {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}} she first appears as the royal cobra who protects the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|qid=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh|1=Pharaoh}} and renews his life in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}.
== Background ==
=== Old Kingdom === Originally Iaret was born as a daughter of {{ill|Set (deity)|en|Set (deity)|qid=Q131795|lt=Seth|1=Set (deity)}}, acting through {{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|qid=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}} as the mother of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}, the sky-deity who is reborn daily as a {{ill|Falke|en|Falke|qid=Q240568|lt=Falcon|1=Falke}} emerging from her {{ill|Eye of Horus|en|Eye of Horus|qid=Q211286|lt=Eye of Horus|1=Eye of Horus}}. The Egyptians further identified Iaret with {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}}, bringer of the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|qid=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile|1=Flooding of the Nile}}. Texts describe Iaret/Sopdet, equated with {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|qid=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}}, as being “taken away and brought back” each year. In {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|qid=Q2445700|lt=Funerary cult|1=Mortuary cult}} liturgy she receives the dead king in her evening barque and guards his reign in the Duat.
With the rise of the sun-god {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} during the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q211001|lt=4th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}}, Iaret’s affiliation shifted: “She is the one who comes forth from Ra and rests upon his brow,” becoming an {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|qid=Q760218|lt=Eye of Ra|1=Eye of Ra}}. Concurrent worship of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} re-cast her as {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|qid=Q79876|lt=Isis|1=Isis}}, protector of the infant Horus who “destroys the confederates of Seth.”
=== Middle Kingdom === In the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} Iaret’s primary seat was in the {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・ジェセル|zh|塔杰塞尔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} regions of the afterlife as the “Radiant Hathor.” She feeds the deceased upon arrival in the Duat until, according to coffin texts, she is “entirely eaten and becomes the body of the deceased.” She therefore “establishes her throne upon the dead” and at {{ill|Sunrise|en|Sunrise|qid=Q193294|lt=Sunrise|1=Sunrise}} transforms back into a cobra at the horizon.
The birthing motif reveals her original role: the dead “come forth from her as the child Horus.” Whereas {{ill|Wadjet|en|Wadjet|qid=Q146007|lt=Wadjet|1=Wadjet}} is placed on the crown, Iaret sits upon the forehead, “causing breath to enter the nostrils” at rebirth.
=== New Kingdom === At the dawn of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} Iaret was revitalised as guardian of king and queen. She gained the epithet “First of the Four Goddesses,” appearing “mighty as {{ill|Tjenenyet|de|Tjenenyet|ja|トジェネネト|zh|特杰涅特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tjenenyet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},” and merging with {{ill|Werethekau|de|Weret Hekau|ja|ウレトヘカウ|zh|韦莱特赫考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Werethekau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tefnut|en|Tefnut|qid=Q116273|lt=Tefnut|1=Tefnut}}.
In royal cult “her throne is upon the queen’s brow.” Iaret is “the goddess whom one acclaims as queen,” while people “spit upon {{ill|Apophis|en|Apophis|qid=Q190511|lt=Apep|1=Apophis}}, her counterpart.”
=== Graeco-Roman Period === During the {{ill|Ptolemaic dynasty|en|Ptolemaic dynasty|qid=Q131976|lt=Egypt in the Hellenistic period|1=Ptolemaic dynasty}} Iaret’s veneration peaked anew. Egyptians perceived her as the primeval goddess {{ill|Neith-hemset|de|Niut-hemset|ja|ニウト＝ヘムセット|zh|尼乌特-赫姆塞特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neith-hemset|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Her name became an {{ill|Epithet|en|Epithet|qid=Q207869|lt=Epithet|1=Epithet}} applied to many deities: Isis, Sopdet, Wadjet, Hathor, {{ill|Mut|en|Mut|qid=Q29409|lt=Mut|1=Mut}}, {{ill|Nekhbet|en|Nekhbet|qid=Q145915|lt=Nekhbet|1=Nekhbet}}, and {{ill|Sekhmet|en|Sekhmet|qid=Q146104|lt=Sekhmet|1=Sekhmet}}.
Royal texts further equate her with the sovereign: “The king offers incense to her,” “The king is her heir,” “The deceased descends as Iaret,” “She protects with her burning breath,” “The {{ill|Sem priest|de|Sem (Priester)|ja|セム祭司|zh|瑟姆祭司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sem priest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} offers incense to her,” “Her eyes are the crowns of the Two Lands,” “She grants health,” and “{{ill|Menhit|en|Menhit|qid=Q45204|lt=Menhit|1=Menhit}} is the Red Crown in the form of Iaret.”
== Iconography == Depictions of Iaret change over Egyptian history: * '''Old Kingdom:''' reared cobra with human head and Hathor-crown. * '''New Kingdom:''' standing human woman with pendant arms and a cobra on her forehead; also as a fire-breathing cobra. * '''Graeco-Roman Period:''' lion-headed serpent wearing the {{ill|Solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|ja|太陽円盤|zh|太阳圆盘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar disk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプト神の一覧|zh|埃及神列表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|qid=Q618140|lt=Sekhet-iaru|1=Aaru}}
== Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Gods and God Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプトの神々事典|zh|埃及诸神与神名词典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Gods and God Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (Leuven: Peeters, 2002), pp. 140–141. ISBN 90-429-1146-8.
{{translated page|de|Iaret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}
